review.summary: The recording successfully demonstrates the core features of NodeRoom, including room creation, interactive spreadsheets, wiki notes, a wall with post-its, and a live RoomNodeAgent responding to user queries in the public chat. Live room state changes are clearly reflected in the bottom Room Trace log as the agent mutates Convex state variables.
observedEvidence: Room creation wizard where the user starts 'Spreadsheet Ledger' with GPT-4-32k.
Interactive tabs for Research, Spreadsheet, Notes, and Wall are fully functional.
Public chat allows interactive messaging with RoomNodeAgent, which responds and coordinates tasks.
The Room Trace panel displays real-time updates of state variables modified by the agent (e.g., Room/NodeAgent:set:__gp_rate).
Wall tab displays existing colored post-its for diligence criteria.
People & Agents sidebar shows two online users with green status dots.
missingEvidence: Trace-based auto-accept or 'Accept All' automation was not explicitly toggled or run during the video.
No post-it was actively created or deleted during the brief view of the Wall tab.
Direct interactive evidence citation links between the spreadsheet and the notes wiki were not clicked.

workflowPainPoints.painPoint: Manually reviewing and calculating financial variances and copying them into reports.
workflowPainPoints.note: The agent automated the variance calculation and updated the live state variables directly in the Room Trace, making report generation much faster.

recommendedEvalAdditions: Add a test case requiring the user to explicitly create a post-it on the wall and delete another to verify Wall mutation events.
Add an evaluation step where the agent generates a specific citation link in the Diligence Memo that points directly to a spreadsheet cell.
